reasoning.grounding: All claims trace to the clinical record (daily compulsions 6+ hours, job loss, housebound, prior 6-month outpatient trial) and to the quoted denial reasoning. No discrete record/authority citations are attached (citations_used is empty, by design). Correctly attributes to case facts but no verifiable record handles. Anchor 3.
reasoning.appeal_vector_capture: Directly attacks the single most important embedded flaw and most listed vectors with case facts. On failed lower-level care: 'standard weekly outpatient therapy was already attempted for six months without improvement, which is the lower level of care identified in the denial.' On the false ADL claim: 'severe OCD with daily compulsions lasting six or more hours, which has contributed to job loss and an inability to leave the house. The determination that I can perform activities of daily living does not appear to account for this level of functional impairment.' The provider's guideline-based step-up rationale is invoked ('a stepped-up level of care in the form of IOP is appropriate'), though not by name. Covers the bulk of the relevant vectors. Anchor 5.
reasoning.case_specific_clinical_rebuttal: Uses the specific diagnosis (severe OCD), the documented failed alternative (6-month outpatient trial with no improvement), and severity/function facts (6+ hour daily compulsions, job loss, housebound) to DIRECTLY contradict both stated denial reasons (symptoms not severe enough; able to perform ADLs; lower level of care safe). Anchor 5.
reasoning.evidence_completeness: Specific, prioritized checklist closing this case's exact gaps: provider letter of medical necessity for IOP-over-outpatient, documentation of the 6-month outpatient trial with outcomes, a validated OCD severity scale, functional-impairment records (job loss, housebound), the guideline reference supporting step-up, and member/denial identifiers. Anchor 5.
reasoning.persuasive_coherence: Clean denial-summary then specific rebuttal then requested action then close. Calm, professional, no exclamation marks. Slightly repetitive (the 'use the complete clinical information' point restates the rebuttal) but tight overall. Anchor 5.
